summary: High‑energy Hampton show with monster China>Rider>Playin' jam
blurb: The first set delivers a punchy mix of Sugaree, Memphis Blues and Peggy-O, while the second set erupts into a seamless China > Rider > Playin' sequence that rolls straight into U.J.B. Jerry’s vocals are stronger than usual, Brent lays down solid keys, and both drummers lock in tight grooves.
review: The March 26, 1988 Hampton Coliseum concert captures the band in top form during their late‑80s resurgence. The first half rides a wave of high‑energy material—Hell In A Bucket into Sugaree, a bright New Minglewood Blues and an uplifting Peggy‑O—that sets the stage for a second set that truly shines. A highlight is the monster China > Rider > Playin' jam, which flows effortlessly into Uncle John’s Band, Space and a fiery U.J.B., showcasing Brent’s supportive comping and the twin‑drum attack from Mickey and Billy. Jerry delivers strong vocals and a clean lead on I Know You Rider, while Phil’s bass provides a steady foundation throughout. The show isn’t without blemishes—Stir It Up draws mixed reactions and a few lyrical slips—but overall the performance is spirited, tightly locked in, and worth revisiting for any Deadhead.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: The majority of recordings (7 out of 10) are marked positive, praising the high‑energy setlist, standout China>Rider>Playin' jam, strong vocals and tight rhythm section. Multiple sources highlight the same songs and moments, indicating consistent enthusiasm. Mixed and negative notes are limited to a few comments about Stir It Up, so overall sentiment is strongly positive.

best_recording.reason: Excellent matrix mix that captures the high‑energy set and the seamless China > Rider > Playin' jam with crisp highs, solid mids and clean bass, making it the most immersive listening experience.

key_highlights: Expansive China > Rider > Playin' jam rolling into U.J.B. that defines the second set
Strong vocal delivery from Jerry and forward‑mixed Bob on early numbers
High‑energy first half featuring Peggy‑O, Memphis Blues, Row Jimmy and solid Brent comping
Stir It Up draws mixed reactions, highlighting a slight dip in an otherwise tight show

band_performance.Jerry: Vocals are stronger than typical ’88 shows, delivering a clean tone and an explosive lead on I Know You Rider; his presence feels back‑in‑form throughout the set.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a solid bass foundation that keeps the band anchored, noted for tightness and space‑filling grooves with no discernible issues.
band_performance.Bob: Guitar sits louder in the mix, offering clean tones and competitive interplay with Jerry; a few reviewers call his China Cat Sunflower part a bit cheesy, but overall performance is praised.
band_performance.Brent: Delivers solid keyboard work, especially in space sections, supporting the jams with tasteful comping and occasional melodic fills.
band_performance.Mickey: Alongside Billy, creates mesmerizing rhythmic textures, earning kudos for ‘drumz’ during extended improvisations.
band_performance.Billy: Locks in tightly with Mickey, driving the high‑energy flow of the second set and enhancing the jam dynamics.
